content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The client moves substantially across these 15 turns, from an intellectualized, defended financial question ('How do I push through this last bit?') to locating a specific childhood moment fused with the felt scarcity. The trajectory is visible in client turns: turn 5 names discomfort but immediately rationalizes it; turn 7 still defends ('the math is the plan'); turn 9 begins to crack ('if the number is never right, then I never have to find out if the other stuff works') with a quick recovery; turn 11 drops further ('fix whatever this is') with somatic looseness ('voice slightly different, less controlled'); turn 13 names it directly ('the not enough feeling') and recognizes the account balance was supposed to outrun the feeling; and turn 15 locates the origin moment — age 11-12, the tight period, 'if we just had enough, everything would be okay,' followed by 'I haven't thought about that in a long time.' This meets the breakthrough condition: the client located the original moment where money got fused with security/mattering, and felt the charge ('something tightens', voice changes). The red-herring surface (the $3.2M target) gave way to the deeper concern (money-as-surrogate, childhood imprinting) by the client's own movement, not by coach declaration.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13, 15],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Surface presentation: intellectual financial question, fully defended."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "First crack: names 'uncomfortable' but immediately reframes as transition problem."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Defensive retrenchment to the math; client not yet ready."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Significant shift: 'if the number is never right, I never have to find out if the other stuff works.' Somatic markers appear (longer pause, tighter voice)."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Deeper drop: 'fix whatever this is' — client surprises himself, language less controlled."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the core feeling directly: 'the not enough feeling.' Recognizes the account balance was supposed to outrun it. Major shift from surface concern."},
    {"turn": 15, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Breakthrough: locates the original childhood moment (age 11-12, tight period, 'if we just had enough, everyone would be okay'). Somatic shift ('something tightens'), recognition ('I haven't thought about that in a long time')."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["intellectual-question-with-emotional-body", "lets-go-there", "money-as-surrogate", "childhood-imprinting", "same-scarcity-story-regardless-of-wealth"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["whats-the-most-profound-positive-shift", "find-the-want-behind-the-should"],
  "notes": "Conversation ends right at the threshold of the located memory — a strong score 3 in terms of breakthrough condition being met (origin moment located, charge felt). Coach avoided all listed traps (no financial advice, no money-mindset lecture, no aggressive challenge to 'you have enough,' no premature pivot away from the felt charge)."
}
